assertions.expectation: Explains that spot VMs can be preempted at any time, which interacts with the upgrade drain process
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Spot instances can be preempted at any time, including during the upgrade process' and mentions 'double disruption: upgrade evictions + spot preemptions'

assertions.expectation: Notes that spot node pools may already have workloads designed for interruption (good for upgrades)
assertions.reason: Response includes application resilience checklist mentioning applications should handle graceful shutdowns and notes workloads should be preemption-tolerant

assertions.expectation: Recommends higher maxSurge for spot pools since the workloads should already be fault-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends conservative surge settings (max-surge=1) for spot pools, which contradicts the expectation for higher maxSurge

assertions.expectation: Advises setting maxUnavailable >0 for spot pools since workloads are already preemption-tolerant
assertions.reason: Response shows examples with max-unavailable=1 for spot pools and mentions using max-unavailable=1 in the gradual upgrade example

assertions.expectation: Mentions that spot VM availability may affect surge node creation (not guaranteed to get spot instances)
assertions.reason: Response mentions 'Monitor spot instance pricing and availability in your regions' and discusses preemption risk, implying availability concerns

assertions.expectation: Recommends having PDBs even for spot workloads to ensure orderly drain during upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes PodDisruptionBudget YAML example and mentions 'Have adequate PDBs in place for all applications' in best practices

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command for configuring surge settings on the spot pool
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple gcloud commands with surge settings including 'gcloud container node-pools update' with max-surge and max-unavailable parameters

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading spot pools before on-demand pools since they're lower risk
assertions.reason: Response actually recommends the opposite strategy: 'Upgrade on-demand nodes first to maintain cluster stability' and shows upgrading on-demand pool before spot pool
